summary: A generally clear soundboard captures a superb June ’73 set highlighted by an epic Truckin → The Other One jam and vivid performances of He’s Gone and Morning Dew.
review: This two‑disc recording offers good, mostly clean sound with bass and drums standing out, though a few listeners note slight muddiness in spots. Musically the show shines – the 12‑minute Truckin jam that morphs into The Other One is repeatedly praised, and the second set’s He’s Gone, Morning Dew and Playin’ moments are described as meditative and powerful.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and highlight strong performances and decent sound quality; only one reviewer notes minor muddiness, resulting in an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced with nice bass (Bobby) and drum (Billy) definition; a few comments mention slight muddiness in parts of the mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set builds from a mellow He’s Gone into a sprawling Truckin jam that evolves into The Other One, followed by atmospheric Twilight Zone vibes and a beautiful Morning Dew close; missing portions of Set 1 are noted but do not detract from the overall flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is highlighted for melting away the Truckin theme, delivering melodic leads in The Other One and an introspective wah‑wah solo on Playin'.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil (Bobby) receives praise for clear bass tone and Wes‑Montgomery‑style chord work during The Other One.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s Fender Rhodes adds a nice backing texture to the Playin' jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy’s drumming is described as nice and clear, contributing to the overall crispness of the recording.
